evidence_quotes.quote: Video games have been a huge part of my life and unfortunately today most games are sold digitally and some games don't even have physical copies anymore.
I have had multiple games i played in the past shut down, both free and games i paid for/paid inside the game for in game content.
So i lost everything i paid for with no offline alternative.
Long story short, please consider adding (digital) videogames to this act.

paragraphs.text: Please consider adding video games as a subject to this act as well. Video games have been a huge part of my life and unfortunately today most games are sold digitally and some games don't even have physical copies anymore. I have had multiple games i played in the past shut down, both free and games i paid for/paid inside the game for in game content. An example for this is a game called zenonzard, i love card games like this and a couple of years ago they suddendly announced that it will be shutting down next month. So i lost everything i paid for with no offline alternative. I agree that supporting a game forever is unreasonable, but that's not what digital ownership is about, it's about getting what you paid for in one way or another. I think the approach of the stop destroying video games citizens initiative (which currently has over 1.4 million signees) is the most reasonable one, developers need to introduce some kind of end of life plan. This shouldn't be expensive if this is taken into account at the start of the video game design process, and the developers should be allowed some liberty in what form they want to make the game available to the consumer who paid for the product. In the end Videogames are a work of art, and it's sad to see that the work of talented artists regularly gets lost to the ages. If a museum finished hosting a rembrandt painting exhibit they don't just burn the painting either. Long story short, please consider adding (digital) videogames to this act. , me and millions of other europeans are very passionate about videogames, and would appreciate it very much if the European Union helped in preserving this art form. Thank you for reading my comment and taking the time to adress this issue.
